title: Write a PROCUREMENT & CONTRACTOR MANAGEMENT PROCEDURE which includes:

turns.speaker: user
turns.text:
Write a PROCUREMENT & CONTRACTOR MANAGEMENT PROCEDURE which includes:

•	Roles and Responsibilities (DOC ID 211 only includes the subcontractors’ responsibilities)
•	Requirement for the contractor/ service provider to complete a hazard assessment as per Element 2 -Hazard Assessment, Analysis and Control; and Element 3 – Controls
•	Occupational Health & Safety criteria for selecting, monitoring and evaluating contractor/ service providers that include i) The ability and competency of the contractor to be able to assess/analyze and control hazards arising from their own work that may impact the organization’s (Urbacon) workers, ii) The ability and competency of the contractor to be able to assess/analyze and control hazards arising from the organizations (Urbacon) work that may impact the contractors workers.
•	Communication with workplace parties when there are changes affecting the health and safety of the work
•	Requirement for the organization (Urbacon) to lead the coordination of their site-specific Occupational Health and Safety requirements on multi-employer workplaces
•	Specific forms for evaluating contractors and service providers

turns.speaker: assistant
turns.text:
PROCUREMENT & CONTRACTOR MANAGEMENT PROCEDURE

1.0 PURPOSE

The purpose of this procedure is to establish a framework and guidelines for the selection, management, and evaluation of contractors and service providers to ensure that Occupational Health & Safety (OH&S) risks are assessed, analyzed, and controlled. This procedure outlines the roles and responsibilities of the stakeholders involved in the procurement and contractor management process and describes the requirements for hazard assessment, communication, and coordination among the different workplace parties.

2.0 SCOPE

This procedure applies to all contractors and service providers who have been engaged by Urbacon for the provision of goods or services on its sites. It also applies to all Urbacon employees, contractors, and subcontractors working on Urbacon’s sites.

3.0 ROLES AND RESPONSIBILITIES

3.1 Urbacon

Urbacon is responsible for:

• Developing and implementing policies and procedures for the management of contractors and service providers
• Establishing OH&S criteria for selecting, monitoring, and evaluating contractors and service providers
• Communicating with contractors and service providers on OH&S matters
• Coordinating the site-specific OH&S requirements of multi-employer workplaces
• Ensuring that all contractors and service providers are aware of the OH&S hazards associated with their work
• Providing contractors and service providers with the necessary resources, training, and communication channels to support the proper management of hazards on-site
• Monitoring and evaluating the OH&S performance of contractors and service providers
• Establishing corrective and preventive actions when contractors and service providers fail to meet OH&S requirements

3.2 Contractors and Service Providers

Contractors and service providers are responsible for:

• Complying with Urbacon’s OH&S policies and procedures
• Assessing, analyzing, and controlling hazards arising from their own work that may impact Urbacon’s workers
• Assessing, analyzing, and controlling hazards arising from Urbacon’s work that may impact their workers
• Reporting any OH&S incidents or hazards to Urbacon
• Participating in OH&S training and communication programs as required by Urbacon
• Providing adequate resources, training, and communication channels to support the proper management of hazards on their work sites
• Complying with any corrective and preventive actions required by Urbacon

4.0 REQUIREMENTS FOR THE CONTRACTOR/ SERVICE PROVIDER TO COMPLETE A HAZARD ASSESSMENT

Contractors and service providers must complete a hazard assessment as per Element 2 -Hazard Assessment, Analysis and Control and Element 3 – Controls. The hazard assessment should consider all potential OH&S hazards associated with their work and outline appropriate controls to manage those hazards.

5.0 OCCUPATIONAL HEALTH & SAFETY CRITERIA FOR SELECTING, MONITORING, AND EVALUATING CONTRACTOR/ SERVICE PROVIDERS

Urbacon must consider the following OH&S criteria when selecting, monitoring, and evaluating contractors and service providers:

• The ability and competency of the contractor/service provider to assess, analyze, and control hazards arising from their own work that may impact Urbacon’s workers.
• The ability and competency of the contractor/ service provider to assess, analyze, and control hazards arising from Urbacon’s work that may impact their workers.
• The contractor/service provider’s previous OH&S performance history
• The contractor/service provider’s OH&S policies, practices and procedures
• The contractor/service provider’s health and safety training programs, including orientation and supervision.
• The adequacy of the contractor/service provider’s OH&S resources, equipment, and facilities.

6.0 COMMUNICATION WITH WORKPLACE PARTIES

Urbacon must communicate with all workplace parties whenever there are changes affecting the health and safety of the work. This communication should be conducted through formal mechanisms such as meetings, orientations, training sessions, and warning signs.

7.0 REQUIREMENT FOR THE ORGANIZATION TO LEAD THE COORDINATION OF THEIR SITE-SPECIFIC OCCUPATIONAL HEALTH AND SAFETY REQUIREMENTS ON MULTI-EMPLOYER WORKPLACES

Urbacon must lead the coordination of its site-specific OH&S requirements for all multi-employer workplaces. Urbacon should inform all contractors and service providers about its site-specific OH&S requirements, including OH&S hazards, controls, and emergency procedures.

8.0 SPECIFIC FORMS FOR EVALUATING CONTRACTORS AND SERVICE PROVIDERS

Urbacon must use specific forms to evaluate contractors and service providers and their OH&S performance. The evaluation criteria should include the OH&S criteria mentioned in section 5.0 and should cover all aspects of the contractor/service provider’s work, including hazard identification, assessment, analysis, and control.

9.0 REFERENCES

• Occupational Health and Safety Act (OHSA) and Regulations
• Urbacon OH&S policies and procedures
• Element 2 -Hazard Assessment, Analysis and Control
• Element 3 – Controls

10.0 APPENDICES

• Appendix 1: Contractor/ Service Provider Hazard Assessment Form
• Appendix 2: Contractor/ Service Provider Evaluation Form.

turns.speaker: user
turns.text: write a Contractor/ Service Provider OHS Evaluation Form
